quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 2 Quiz 11
quiz.subtitle: Mixed Review of Reasoning and Proof Concepts
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 2.10 Problem-Solving with Reasoning and Proof

questions.question: The first four terms of a pattern are $4, 11, 25, 53$. Using inductive reasoning, what is the fifth term?
questions.explanation: The pattern multiplies the previous term by $2$ and adds $3$: $4(2) + 3 = 11$, $11(2) + 3 = 25$, $25(2) + 3 = 53$. Applying the rule: $53(2) + 3 = 109$.

questions.question: Consider the true statement: "If an integer ends in $0$, then it is divisible by $5$." What is the truth value of its inverse?
options: True, because all integers divisible by $5$ end in $0$
True, because any integer ending in $5$ is also divisible by $5$
False, because $35$ is divisible by $5$ but does not end in $0$
False, because $40$ is divisible by $5$ and ends in $0$
questions.explanation: The inverse is "If an integer does not end in $0$, then it is not divisible by $5$." This is false; $35$ does not end in $0$ yet is divisible by $5$, providing a counterexample.

questions.question: Given: "If a triangle is equilateral, then it is isosceles" and "If a triangle is isosceles, then it has at least two congruent sides." If $\triangle ABC$ is equilateral, what conclusion follows?
options: It has exactly three congruent sides by the Law of Detachment
It has at least two congruent sides by the Law of Detachment
It has exactly three congruent sides by the Law of Syllogism
It has at least two congruent sides by the Law of Syllogism
questions.explanation: By the Law of Syllogism, the two conditionals combine to: "If a triangle is equilateral, then it has at least two congruent sides." Since $\triangle ABC$ is equilateral, the conclusion follows by the Law of Detachment.

questions.question: A proof moves from $5(x - 3) = 45$ to $5x - 15 = 45$. Which property justifies this step?
questions.explanation: The Distributive Property allows multiplying each term inside parentheses by the factor outside: $5(x - 3) = 5x - 15$.

questions.question: In a proof, $JK \cong LM$ and $LM \cong NP$. Which property justifies the conclusion that $JK \cong NP$?
questions.explanation: The Transitive Property of Congruence states that if two figures are each congruent to a third figure, then they are congruent to each other. Both $JK$ and $NP$ are congruent to $LM$.

questions.question: Ray $BD$ bisects $\angle ABC$. If $m\angle ABD = 3x + 14$ and $m\angle DBC = 5x - 12$, what is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: An angle bisector divides an angle into two congruent parts, so $3x + 14 = 5x - 12$. Solving: $14 = 2x - 12$, so $26 = 2x$, giving $x = 13$.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. Consecutive interior angles measure $(4x + 11)^\circ$ and $(2x + 13)^\circ$. What is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: When parallel lines are cut by a transversal, consecutive interior angles are supplementary: $(4x + 11) + (2x + 13) = 180$. Simplifying: $6x + 24 = 180$, so $6x = 156$, giving $x = 26$.

questions.question: Two lines are cut by a transversal. Alternate exterior angles measure $(7x - 19)^\circ$ and $(5x + 15)^\circ$. What value of $x$ proves the lines are parallel?
questions.explanation: By the converse of the Alternate Exterior Angles Theorem, the lines are parallel if the alternate exterior angles are congruent: $7x - 19 = 5x + 15$. Solving: $2x = 34$, giving $x = 17$.

questions.question: Line $p$ intersects line $q$ at a right angle. One angle formed is given by $6x - 12$. What is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: Perpendicular lines form right angles, each measuring $90^\circ$. Setting the expression equal to $90$: $6x - 12 = 90$, so $6x = 102$, giving $x = 17$.

questions.question: Two supplementary angles are such that the larger is $20^\circ$ more than three times the smaller. What is the measure of the larger angle?
questions.explanation: Let the smaller angle be $x$. Then the larger is $3x + 20$. Since they are supplementary: $x + (3x + 20) = 180$, so $4x + 20 = 180$, giving $x = 40$. The larger angle is $3(40) + 20 = 140^\circ$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a strong command of reasoning, proof techniques, and their application across the full range of Unit 2 concepts.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand most concepts but review the converses of parallel line theorems and the distinction between the Law of Detachment and Law of Syllogism.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: inductive vs. deductive reasoning, conditional statements and their related forms, algebraic properties of equality, and angle relationships with parallel lines.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Review each section: inductive reasoning and conjectures, conditional statements, deductive reasoning with Detachment and Syllogism, algebraic properties, two-column proofs, and parallel/perpendicular line theorems.
